content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print all prime numbers in the range [LLL, RRR].


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains two space separated integers, LLL and RRR - The start and end of the range.


Output Format

Print on a single line, all prime numbers between LLL and RRR (inclusive) in increasing order with a space between them.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space separated integers L and R (1 ≤ L ≤ R ≤ 1000) denoting the inclusive range for that test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing all prime numbers between L and R inclusive, in increasing order, separated by a single space. If there is no prime in the interval, output an empty line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case. The primes between 2 and 11 inclusive are 2, 3, 5, 7 and 11.
Only the numbers 11 and 13 are prime in the given range, so they are printed separated by a space.
The range consists of a single number 5, which is prime, therefore the output is just "5".

solution.approach: Pre‑compute primality for every integer up to the maximum possible R (1000) using the Sieve of Eratosthenes. The sieve runs once in O(MAX log log MAX) time and O(MAX) memory. For each test case iterate from L to R and output the numbers whose pre‑computed flag is true. This yields overall O(T·(R‑L+1)) time which is trivial for the given limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(MAX log log MAX + total length of all queried intervals) where MAX=1000. With the constraints this is well within limits.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(MAX) for the boolean array storing primality (≈1001 bytes).

code.Python3:
import sys

def sieve(limit):
    prime=[True]*(limit+1)
    prime[0]=prime[1]=False
    for p in range(2,int(limit**0.5)+1):
        if prime[p]:
            for q in range(p*p,limit+1,p):
                prime[q]=False
    return prime

prime=sieve(1000)

data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
it=iter(data)
T=int(next(it))
out_lines=[]
for _ in range(T):
    L=int(next(it)); R=int(next(it))
    line=[str(i) for i in range(L,R+1) if prime[i]]
    out_lines.append(' '.join(line))
print('\n'.join(out_lines))
